reasoning:
The reference average corresponds exactly to 3 fatalities in the previous 360 days in Belarus (averaging 0.25 per 30-day period). Since fatalities in ACLED must be integers, the question resolves YES only if there is at least 1 fatality in the 30-day window leading up to the resolution date. Assuming independence, a Poisson distribution with an expected value of 0.25 per month yields a ~22% chance of 1 or more fatalities in a typical 30-day period. If the 3 historical fatalities were physically clustered (e.g. from a single explosion or drone strike), the baseline chance of a fatal event in any 30-day period would be even lower, closer to 10-15%. 

For the first date (June 1), we are 24 days into the 30-day window. Extensive searches yield no recent reports of ACLED-qualifying fatalities in Belarus during May 2025, suggesting a very low probability for this period unless an event goes unreported or occurs in the remaining 6 days. For the subsequent resolution dates, the probability slightly regress back to the reference base rate (20-22%), while uncertainty steadily pushes the estimates closer to 50% for more distant timelines (since the Ukraine-Russia war or post-election crackdowns could trigger unforeseen escalations).
